message:
refactor(mcp): share the CLI write-lock lifecycle with MCP mutating tools

Previously the cross-process write lock (.beads/.write.lock) and the
auto-flush sync lock (.beads/.sync.lock) lived as private helpers in
src/main.rs (blocking_write_lock / try_sync_lock). The CLI used them
on every mutating command, but the MCP server bypassed them and opened
its own SQLite connection directly per request. That meant MCP
handlers could race against a concurrent CLI invocation on the same
beads repo, which is exactly the scenario the flock was added to
prevent (issue #243).

Changes:

  * src/sync/mod.rs - Hoist blocking_write_lock and try_sync_lock out
    of main.rs into the sync module and make them pub. Same fast-path
    try_lock + blocking fallback semantics as before, with
    tracing::debug on the contended path. Both functions are #[must_use]
    because dropping the returned File releases the lock.

  * src/main.rs - Remove the local copies and route the call sites
    through beads_rust::sync::blocking_write_lock /
    beads_rust::sync::try_sync_lock. Also: the write lock now gates on
    needs_write_lock(&cli.command) instead of the coarser is_mutating
    flag, so non-mutating-but-flagged operations don't serialize
    unnecessarily.

  * src/mcp/mod.rs - Extend BeadsState with the two fields needed to
    drive auto-flush from inside handlers: jsonl_path: PathBuf and
    allow_external_jsonl: bool. run_serve computes
    implicit_external_jsonl_allowed(...) up front and stores it on the
    shared state. New method BeadsState::with_mutation(|storage| {...})
    wraps the standard CLI mutation lifecycle: acquire the blocking
    write lock, open a fresh SqliteStorage, run the caller's closure,
    then acquire the non-blocking sync lock and call auto_flush.
    Auto-flush failures are logged at debug level and do not fail the
    mutation.

  * src/mcp/tools.rs - Rewire every mutating MCP handler through
    with_mutation: CreateIssueTool (now includes parent validation, ID
    generation with parent/next-child-number path that avoids hash
    collisions, issue insert, label attach, and parent-child dep link
    all in one locked closure), plus the issue update, close, and
    dep add/remove handlers. All of these now share the same locking
    and auto-flush guarantees as the CLI, so mixed CLI + MCP workloads
    on the same .beads/ directory stay consistent.

Net effect: MCP handlers and CLI commands are now indistinguishable
from the storage's point of view - same write lock, same auto-flush,
same guarantees.
